refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these should be separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide accurate terrain data of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Develop a unified worksite model integrating terrain and operational feature data
Produce separate terrain and feature representations accessible via a common interface
selected_dp.selected_dp: Produce separate terrain and feature representations accessible via a common interface

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By separating terrain representation from feature annotations but providing a common access interface, the design supports modularity and independence, reducing coupling risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and feature data sources
Risk that integration interface may become a bottleneck if not well designed
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume separate data representations
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the interface requirements between terrain and feature data representations to ensure modular access and minimize coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing environment data representations including terrain and feature models

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides sensor data input and status output related to environment mapping

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to update or query terrain and feature representations

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain and feature data are core to NGHE's environment modeling; minimal diffusion since data is accessed via common interface within this module.
Vehicle module uses environment data but does not own or produce separate terrain and feature representations; would cause diffusion across modules.
Teleoperation consumes environment data for remote control but does not produce or maintain separate representations; high diffusion if contained here.
Safety Services may use environment data for alerts but is not responsible for producing or managing terrain/feature models; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of producing separate terrain and feature representations accessible via a common interface, as it directly manages environment data and can provide a unified interface internally, minimizing diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Risk of interface complexity if common interface is not carefully designed to serve diverse consumers.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to produce separate terrain and feature representations accessible via a common interface directly addresses the FR requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation. This mapping clearly defines what is needed (FR) and how it will be logically solved (DP) with a focus on separate but accessible data representations.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary to contain the DP, showing high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk within this module. The common interface approach reduces diffusion risk by centralizing access to terrain and feature data. However, care must be taken to manage interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to avoid diffusion across module boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module can be tested to confirm it produces and provides access to the required terrain and feature representations, which can then be consumed by planning and execution functions as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with ownership and responsibilities explicitly defined, minimizing ambiguity in implementation.

recommended_revisions: Define and document the common interface carefully to ensure it meets the needs of all consumers and minimizes complexity.
Establish clear interface contracts and boundaries between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to mitigate coupling and diffusion risks.

Include verification steps in the test plan to confirm that the NGHE Solution module produces the required worksite model/map and that it is accessible to planning and execution functions as specified.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current FR to DP to IM mapping is sound and feasible. The DP provides a clear logical solution to the FR, and the IM boundary is well chosen with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition of the FR into more detailed sub-FRs is warranted to ensure thorough coverage and detailed design. This will help in managing complexity, verifying safety requirements, and clarifying interface definitions.
next_level_sub_frs: Provide common interface for terrain and feature data access
Ensure data accessibility to planning and execution functions

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be further decomposed to handle sub-functions.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be clearly defined and managed.
Verification plans will incorporate tests for sub-FRs and their implementations.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Complexity in defining and maintaining the common interface.
Ensuring verification coverage for all sub-FRs.
